summary: Nearly matched sysdolphin/baselib/controller.c by implementing major controller queue, raw status, master status, clamp, analog-to-digital conversion, scale, and init logic. The PR also corrected controller-related types and prototypes in headers, added a small math/util helper for matching atan2/fabs behavior, and typed the game main controller queue as HSD_PadData[5]. The PR body explicitly notes two remaining non-matches: a swapped copy issue in HSD_PadRenewRawStatus and a stack issue in HSD_PadADConvertCheck1.
what_changed: Implemented HSD_PadRenewRawStatus(bool err_check), including rumble interpretation, PADRead into a local PADStatus[4], queue-full behavior by qtype, queue shifting/merging, PADReset mask construction for err == -1, and reset-switch edge tracking.

Implemented HSD_PadRenewMasterStatus, consuming queued raw PADStatus entries under disabled interrupts, converting them into HSD_PadMasterStatus entries, applying clamp, AD conversion, scaling, cross-direction logic, and trigger/release/repeat bookkeeping.
Added static controller helpers HSD_PadClampCheck3, HSD_PadClamp, HSD_PadADConvertCheck1, HSD_PadADConvert, and HSD_PadScale.

Added HSD_PadInit, initializing HSD_PadLibData from default_libinfo_data, setting queue metadata, initializing rumble, filling master/copy/game status arrays with default_status_data, and calling PADInit.
Added default_status_data, default_libinfo_data, and pad_bit globals in controller.c.
Changed HSD_PadClampCheck1 from externally visible to static.
Corrected HSD_PadData by removing the rumble_mask field from controller.h.
Adjusted PadLibData.adc_type from u8 to s8.
Changed HSD_PadRenewRawStatus prototype from int parameter to bool.
Changed HSD_PadInit prototype from loosely typed integer/u8 pointer arguments to u8 qnum, HSD_PadData* queue, u16 nb_list, and HSD_PadRumbleListData* listdatap.
Changed PADRead prototype in dolphin/pad/pad.h from void PADRead(u8*) to void PADRead(PADStatus*), matching the controller implementation's local PADStatus array usage.
Changed gmMain_8046B108 from raw u8[0xF0] storage to typed HSD_PadData[5].
Added a static inline double fabs(double f) wrapper around __fabs in src/MSL/math.h.
Added atan2f_check(s8 y, s8 x) to util.h, using fabs(x) == 0.0 to return +/- pi/2 and otherwise calling atan2f(y, x).
smart_moves: Improved matching by replacing raw byte storage with HSD_PadData[5], making the queue type reflect how controller init and queue access actually use the buffer.
Tightened function prototypes to use domain-specific types, especially PADRead(PADStatus*) and HSD_PadInit(HSD_PadData*), reducing casts and helping the compiler produce expected code.
Moved small controller-only helper HSD_PadClampCheck1 to static visibility, suggesting it is an internal implementation detail rather than a public API.
Used default struct instances for PadLibData and HSD_PadStatus initialization, which can better match original data initialization patterns than assigning fields one by one.
Introduced pad_bit[4] as a table of PAD_CHAN*_BIT values, matching repeated channel-to-mask logic cleanly in HSD_PadRenewRawStatus.
Factored angle calculation through atan2f_check to handle x == 0 explicitly, likely to reproduce original behavior/codegen around vertical stick directions.
Preserved interrupt safety in status renewal by wrapping queue consumption in OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts.
decomp_lessons: For controller/input decompilation, correct struct typing can be as important as function body reconstruction; changing gmMain_8046B108 from u8[0xF0] to HSD_PadData[5] and correcting HSD_PadData layout directly supports matching pointer arithmetic.

Header prototypes should be corrected as soon as evidence supports them. PADRead(PADStatus*) and HSD_PadInit(HSD_PadData*) make downstream controller code cleaner and less dependent on placeholder byte pointers.
Global default data blobs can be represented as initialized structs when the original appears to copy defaults into runtime state.
Stick clamping and analog-to-digital conversion depend on exact math helper behavior, float constants, and stack/codegen quirks; helper wrappers like atan2f_check and fabs may be needed for matching.
Queue code around controller input is sensitive to qread/qwrite/qcount/qnum interactions; preserving the original shift/merge order matters for both behavior and matching.

When a PR is only a near match, explicitly documenting the remaining non-matches is valuable for future agents. Here, the remaining issues were HSD_PadRenewRawStatus copy swapping and HSD_PadADConvertCheck1 stack behavior.
naming_conventions: Controller functions use the HSD_Pad prefix, e.g. HSD_PadRenewRawStatus, HSD_PadRenewMasterStatus, HSD_PadClamp, HSD_PadScale, and HSD_PadADConvert.
Internal helper checks use numbered suffixes matching inferred original names, e.g. HSD_PadClampCheck1, HSD_PadClampCheck3, and HSD_PadADConvertCheck1.
Controller status globals are named HSD_PadMasterStatus, HSD_PadCopyStatus, and HSD_PadGameStatus.
Default data globals use descriptive lower-case names: default_status_data and default_libinfo_data.
PadLibData fields use subsystem prefixes for grouped settings, such as adc_type, adc_th, adc_angle, clamp_stickType, clamp_stickShift, clamp_analogLRMin, and scale_analogAB.
Game main unnamed/static data kept its decomp symbol gmMain_8046B108 but gained a real type, HSD_PadData[5].
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK(8) inside HSD_PadADConvertCheck1, indicating stack-shape manipulation was needed, though the PR body says a stack issue still remained there.
Used explicit float/double constants for angular sectors in HSD_PadADConvertCheck1, including pi/4, 3pi/4, and +/-pi/2 style values, to match original branch thresholds.
Used sqrtf and atan2f_check from MSL/trigf-related headers for stick magnitude and direction codegen.
Added an inline double fabs wrapper in MSL/math.h around __fabs so fabs(x) could be used in inline matching contexts.
Kept queue operations close to original control flow, including a goto skip for qtype 2 in HSD_PadRenewRawStatus.
Copied PADStatus entries into the queue with a simple for loop and post-incremented qwrite pointer, which may influence generated stores and pointer arithmetic.
Used bool for err_check in HSD_PadRenewRawStatus, likely matching original boolean conditional codegen better than int.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body records two known remaining issues: HSD_PadRenewRawStatus has a copy that is being swapped around, and HSD_PadADConvertCheck1 has a stack issue.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching HSD_PadRenewRawStatus to see how the swapped copy issue was resolved.
Look for later PRs touching HSD_PadADConvertCheck1 or PAD_STACK to see how the stack mismatch was fixed.
Search for subsequent changes to HSD_PadData layout, especially the removed rumble_mask field.
Search for later controller.c matching status to confirm whether this PR's helper structure and prototypes survived final matching.
Search for uses of atan2f_check and fabs inline wrappers in other baselib math-heavy matches.
